item.title: Bioengineered AAV9 and Optimised Microdystrophin Vectors Augment
Development of Emodin Nanocrystal-Loaded Hydrogel Patch for Rapid Wound Repair
Dainik Jagran: Potassium Ferric Oxalate Nanoparticles to Prevent Blood Clots
item.description:
Featured in Dainik Jagran — Scientists at the School of Biomedical Engineering, IIT (BHU), have developed a novel nanomaterial — Potassium Ferric Oxalate nanoparticles — that effectively prevents blood clot formation (thrombosis) without any side effects.

The research team, led by Dr. Sudip Mukherjee, demonstrated that:

  • The nanoparticles prevent blood clotting for up to 48 hours
  • No adverse effects on blood cell morphology, size, or structure were observed
  • Effective prevention of thrombosis was also demonstrated in mouse models
  • The research has been published in ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces
  • A patent has been filed for this breakthrough

This innovation overcomes the limitations of traditional blood-thinning drugs like Warfarin and Heparin, which are associated with side effects such as excessive bleeding and bone weakness.

item.title: Hindustan Daily: IIT's Nanoparticle Will Prevent Blood Clot Formation
item.description:
Featured in Hindustan (Apne Yuva section) — Researchers at IIT (BHU) have developed Potassium Ferric Oxalate nanoparticles that prevent blood clot formation.

Key highlights from the coverage:

  • Reduces the risk of stroke, heart attack, and pulmonary embolism
  • Successfully prevented blood clot formation for up to 48 hours
  • Published in the prestigious international journal ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces
  • Patent has been filed for the innovation

The research was led by Dr. Sudip Mukherjee, Assistant Professor at the School of Biomedical Engineering, IIT (BHU), with Ph.D. scholar Devyani Yenurkar as a key contributor.

item.title: IIT BHU Breakthrough: KFeOx Nanoparticles Prevent Blood Clotting
item.description:
Researchers at IIT (BHU), led by Dr. Sudip Mukherjee, have developed Potassium Ferric Oxalate Nanoparticles (KFeOx-NPs) — a novel nanomaterial that prevents blood clotting without altering blood structure.

This breakthrough has the potential to be a game-changer in diagnostics and treatment of thrombotic disorders including:

  • Stroke
  • Heart attack
  • Pulmonary embolism
  • Deep vein thrombosis

The KFeOx-NPs represent a significant advancement over conventional anticoagulants, offering an effective and safe approach to preventing dangerous blood clots that are among the leading causes of death worldwide.

item.title: IIT BHU Research: Nanoparticles Prevent Blood Clotting for 48 Hours
item.description:
A major research breakthrough at IIT (BHU), Varanasi — scientists have discovered a novel nanoparticle that prevents blood clot formation (thrombosis) for up to 48 hours without altering blood structure or properties.

The research, led by Dr. Sudip Mukherjee’s team at the School of Biomedical Engineering, has been published in the international journal ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces, and a patent has been filed for the discovery.

This nanoparticle-based approach offers a safer and more effective alternative for the prevention and diagnosis of blood-related diseases such as stroke, heart attack, pulmonary embolism, and deep vein thrombosis.

item.title: Engineered cell-laden hemostatic hydrogels for all-round rapid repair of diabetic wounds
Potassium ferric oxalate nanoparticles prevent human blood clotting and thrombosis in a mouse model
Advancement in ceramic biomaterials for dental implants
